# Temporary CLI Session Email Evidence Memo

## Scope

This memo identifies the strongest workspace-local email messages currently available in the migrated prior-research packet. It is intended to tighten the complaint by tying protected complaints, voucher reversal, accommodation handling, and documentation-escalation allegations to specific exported message files already present in the workspace.

## Core Message Exhibits

| Memo ID | Source File | Date | Sender | Key Proposition Supported |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| EMAIL-01 | `temporary-cli-session-migration/prior-research-results/emergency_motion_packet/exhibits/Exhibit J - starworks5-additional-info-import/0012-Re-Additional-Information-Needed-CAMTdTS_FhDVzVctv75ZTfNndnL9QiuZyzEkD9bZ8-OKR7mZk6g-mail.gmail.com/message.json` | February 26, 2026 | Benjamin Barber | Written protected complaint asserting intent to sue under the Civil Rights Act, Blossom non-processing, service-animal refusal, race discrimination, and delay caused by demands for stale financial documents and a one-bedroom placement. |
| EMAIL-02 | `temporary-cli-session-migration/prior-research-results/emergency_motion_packet/exhibits/Exhibit J - starworks5-additional-info-import/0012-Re-Additional-Information-Needed-CAMTdTS_FhDVzVctv75ZTfNndnL9QiuZyzEkD9bZ8-OKR7mZk6g-mail.gmail.com/message.json` | February 25, 2026 chain preserved in same thread | Kati Tilton and Benjamin Barber | HACC requested tax, crypto, banking, and asset records tied to multiple entities, and Benjamin Barber responded that many demanded records concerned defunct entities or non-existent accounts. Supports the documentation-escalation theory. |
| EMAIL-03 | `temporary-cli-session-migration/prior-research-results/emergency_motion_packet/exhibits/Exhibit M - starworks5-ktilton-orientation-import/0007-RE-HCV-Orientation-f9ca224dab4a4487bb20b9f7ff33afbe-clackamas.us/message.json` | March 19, 2026 | Kati Tilton | HACC stated that the subsidy worksheet and voucher issuance for a two-bedroom unit had been created and sent in error and that a one-bedroom voucher would issue that day. |
| EMAIL-04 | `temporary-cli-session-migration/prior-research-results/emergency_motion_packet/exhibits/Exhibit M - starworks5-ktilton-orientation-import/0009-RE-HCV-Orientation-0cb1a9bce9ce436ba29b873de4aef556-clackamas.us/message.json` | March 20, 2026 | Kati Tilton, quoting Benjamin Barber's same-day reply | HACC stated that the voucher could be reissued as a two-bedroom if the reasonable accommodation were approved, while the embedded reply states that an appointment had already been scheduled to seek a two-bedroom accommodation. |

## High-Value Excerpts

### EMAIL-01 / EMAIL-02: February 26 protected complaint and escalation thread

The exported message states that Benjamin Barber had already indicated an intent to sue under the Civil Rights Act, had stated an intention to leave Clackamas County, and alleged that Blossom had refused to process applications for two months, had refused to house a service animal, and had engaged in race discrimination. The same message also alleges intentional delay through demands for financial documents tied to companies that had not existed for years and through pressure to accept a one-bedroom arrangement.

The preserved February 25 chain inside the same exported message also records Kati Tilton's request for proof of 2025 tax filings for multiple businesses, crypto statements, all bank statements, winnings information, and other asset verification. That chain materially strengthens the complaint's allegation that documentation demands escalated after protected complaints and impeded relocation.

### EMAIL-03: March 19 voucher reversal

The March 19, 2026 message from Kati Tilton states: "The subsidy worksheet and voucher issuance for a 2bdrm was created and sent in error. The attached are for a one-bedroom voucher that will be issued today." This is the cleanest currently located documentary support for the allegati
